1.11a: Instructions must be accessed via the LMS Conveyancer Zone website.
The borrower's copy of the mortgage offer constitutes the formal instruction.
Mortgage offer documents require borrower signature.
A 7-day reflection period applies, which the borrower can waive.
Use standard 'Mortgage Deed' when Mortgagors and Borrowers are identical.
Use 'JMSO Mortgage Deed' when Mortgagors and Borrowers are not identical.

5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm subscribes to the Search Code monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for remortgages if the lender is protected and title is certified marketable.
Search insurance is accepted for purchases if the lender is protected, title is certified marketable, and risks are explained to borrowers.

5.5.3c: Building regulation consents not required; borrower should retain original; firm must keep a copy.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use not required; borrower should retain original; firm must keep a copy.

5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not acceptable security.

5.8.1: Buildings converted into max 4 flats are accepted if the borrower occupies one, owns the freehold, and other flats are on long leases.
5.8.5: One of two leasehold flats accepted if security includes both the leasehold interest and the freehold reversion.

5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be 50 years at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: Loans Underwriting Department, Ebbisham House, 30 Church Street, Epsom, Surrey KT17 4NL; Email: loansunderwriting@ncbs.co.uk
Ground rent at lease start must not exceed 0.1% of property value or £500 per annum, whichever is higher.
Ground rent must remain reasonable; RPI-linked escalation is acceptable.
Unreasonable multipliers (e.g., doubling every 5, 10, or 15 years) are not permitted and must be referred.
Leases must be amended to comply with ground rent caps or the case cannot proceed.
5.14.10: Loans Underwriting Department, Ebbisham House, 30 Church Street, Epsom, Surrey KT17 4NL
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service should be retained by the borrower and firm; do not send to lender.
5.15.2a: Estate rent charges are acceptable if Section 121 of the Law of Property Act is excluded, a 28-day mortgagee protection clause exists, or the owner is a resident-owned management company.
Deed of variation is required if above conditions are not met.
Indemnity policy is not an acceptable alternative for non-compliant estate rent charges.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates should be retained by the borrower and firm; do not send to lender.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association should be retained by the borrower and firm; do not send to lender.
5.16.2: Loans Underwriting Department, Ebbisham House, 30 Church Street, Epsom, Surrey KT17 4NL
5.17.5a: Power of attorney should be retained on the firm's file; do not send to lender.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation should be retained on the firm's file; do not send to lender.
5.19.1: Lender does not provide loans for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership schemes.
5.20.1: Report to lender if solar PV panel lease fails UK Finance minimum requirements; provide copy of lease and details of non-compliance.
5.20.3: Buildings insurer must be notified of solar panel installation.
All appropriate consents must be obtained.
Copy of solar panel lease must be provided on completion with title deeds.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed to the lender.
